steps.title: Rearrange to standard form
steps.content: Move all terms to one side: $4x^2 - x = 18$ becomes $4x^2 - x - 18 = 0$. The coefficients are $a = 4$, $b = -1$, and $c = -18$.

steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula to find both solutions
steps.content:
Using the quadratic formula $x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$ with our coefficients $a = 4$, $b = -1$, $c = -18$:

$$x = \frac{-(-1) \pm \sqrt{(-1)^2 - 4(4)(-18)}}{2(4)}$$

Simplify inside the square root: $(-1)^2 - 4(4)(-18) = 1 + 288 = 289 = 17^2$

$$x = \frac{1 \pm 17}{8}$$

This gives two solutions:
  • $x = \frac{1 + 17}{8} = \frac{18}{8} = \frac{9}{4}$ (positive)
  • $x = \frac{1 - 17}{8} = \frac{-16}{8} = -2$ (negative)

The positive solution is $\frac{9}{4}$ or \$2.25$.
